subject: Physics
chapter: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits

data.markdown:
# 3. SHORT NOTES (QUICK REVISION)

## 3.1 Semiconductors
- **Semiconductor:** Material with electrical conductivity between conductor and insulator.
- **Examples:** Germanium, Silicon, Gallium Arsenide.
- **Properties:** High resistance at low temperature, low resistance at high temperature.

## 3.2 Intrinsic Semiconductors
- **Intrinsic Semiconductor:** Pure semiconductor material.
- **Examples:** Germanium, Silicon.
- **Charge Carriers:** Electrons and holes.
- **Conductivity:** Due to thermal excitation.

## 3.3 Extrinsic Semiconductors
- **Extrinsic Semiconductor:** Doped semiconductor material.
- **Types:** P-type and N-type.
- **Doping:** Introducing impurities to increase conductivity.
- **P-type Doping:** Group III impurity (Boron).
- **N-type Doping:** Group V impurity (Phosphorus).

## 3.4 Diodes
- **Diode:** P-N junction device.
- **Types:** PN, NPN, PNP.
- **Diode Characteristics:** Forward and reverse bias.
- **Forward Bias:** Positive voltage at anode, negative at cathode.
- **Reverse Bias:** Negative voltage at anode, positive at cathode.

## 3.5 Zener Diode
- **Zener Diode:** Special type of PN diode.
- **Zener Breakdown:** High reverse voltage, high current.
- **Zener Diode Applications:** Voltage regulation, voltage reference.

## 3.6 Rectifiers
- **Rectifier:** Device that converts AC to DC.
- **Types:** Half-wave, Full-wave.
- **Rectifier Circuit:** Uses diode(s) to rectify AC voltage.

## 3.7 Clipper Circuits
- **Clipper Circuit:** Circuit that clips off a portion of AC waveform.
- **Types:** Half-wave and Full-wave.
- **Clipper Circuit Applications:** Removing unwanted signals.

## 3.8 Clamp Circuits
- **Clamp Circuit:** Circuit that clamps a portion of AC waveform.
- **Types:** Half-wave and Full-wave.
- **Clamp Circuit Applications:** Limiting voltage levels.

## 3.9 Logic Gates
- **Logic Gate:** Digital circuit that performs logical operations.
- **Types:** AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR.
- **Logic Gate Applications:** Digital electronics, computer circuits.

## 3.10 Transistors
- **Transistor:** Three-layer device.
- **Types:** Bipolar, Field Effect.
- **Transistor Applications:** Amplification, switching, voltage regulation.

## 3.11 Transistor Amplifiers
- **Transistor Amplifier:** Circuit that amplifies weak signals.
- **Types:** Common Emitter, Common Collector.
- **Transistor Amplifier Applications:** Audio amplifiers, radio transmitters.

## 3.12 Transistor Switches
- **Transistor Switch:** Circuit that switches on/off a load.
- **Types:** Digital and Analog.
- **Transistor Switch Applications:** Power supplies, motor control.

## 3.13 Oscillators
- **Oscillator:** Circuit that generates a repetitive signal.
- **Types:** LC, RC, Crystal.
- **Oscillator Applications:** Timekeeping, frequency control.

## 3.14 Multivibrators
- **Multivibrator:** Circuit that generates two stable states.
- **Types:** Astable, Monostable, Bistable.
- **Multivibrator Applications:** Timekeeping, memory circuits.
